_doc: Tempdoc 609 Phase 6 — surface task-state retention teeth + durability declaration. Makes the M1 defect (a surface destroying its own recoverable task state as a navigation side effect) UNREPRESENTABLE: a recoverable-store destroyer call inside a view's lifecycle callback fails the build. Clearing recoverable task state must be intent-driven (an explicit user action handler), never a connect/disconnect side effect. `recoverableStores` is the consumed durability declaration (609 §D.2): which singleton stores hold recoverable task state and at what durability — `url` (also reload/share-survivable via a StateBinding) or `memory` (navigation-survivable only). This register replaces an unconsumed `durability` wire field on StateBinding (substrate-without-consumer-flavors): the gate is the consumer, and durability is declared where it is enforced.

forbiddenDestroyers.why: an empty-string setQuery wipes the WHOLE shared search snapshot (query + results + counts + trace + facets) — the original 609 bug. Clearing belongs to the explicit in-box clear / Esc handler, not a lifecycle callback.
double-quoted form of the same empty-query wipe.
wipes the recoverable composer draft + mode. Clearing belongs to the explicit New chat action (newConversation), not connectedCallback.

recoverableStores.note: query is URL-projected via the StateBinding /query→search; results/counts/trace/facets are memory-recoverable (re-derived from query on reload, retained across navigation by the singleton).
composer draft (query) + affordance (mode) are URL-projectable and survive navigation via the singleton; reset only on explicit New chat.

per-tab sessionStorage pointer; provides RELOAD restore of the chat thread (fresh instance, empty thread → reload via the pointer). Same-session navigation needs no store — the chat thread survives as retained instance @state (see instanceRetained).

instanceRetained.note: Most recoverable task state needs NO store: the Stage retains each surface's element instance across navigation (chrome/Shell.ts _surfaceElCache, append-only), so component @state survives a tab switch by construction while streams still tear down on disconnect. This covers, for free: search selection + every surface's drafts/expanded-trees/sub-tab (PresentationEditor JSON draft, Settings theme/rename/delete-confirm drafts, Library folder/excludes drafts, Browse tree+filter, Brain sections, etc.). The ONLY non-free piece is DOM scroll offset (resets on re-attach), saved/restored via a per-surface instance field (e.g. SearchSurface.savedScrollTop). Stores remain ONLY for cross-boundary durability: url-projected fields (reload/share) + the lastViewedConversation reload pointer.

_settleScopeDoc: Settle-coverage applies ONLY to surfaces the Stage RETAINS (mounted via the surface catalog). A view file is in scope iff the `customElements.define('jf-…')` tag it defines appears as a `mountTag` in `retainedSurfaceManifest`. Conversation-shape views (NavigateView/SummarizeView, registered via viewFactoryRegistry, NOT catalog mountTags) and other non-stage-mounted sub-views are excluded — they are destroyed normally on navigation, so they have no instance-retention settle obligation.
_settleDoc: Tempdoc 609 settle-coverage — the SYMMETRIC half of this gate. Because instance-retention keeps ALL component @state across navigation, TRANSIENT @state (in-flight flags, errors, partial buffers, transient feedback) must be RESET on hide via the surface's `settleTransients()` (the JfElement seam), else a stale spinner / error survives a tab switch (the working rule's 'reset by default' list). The gate flags any reactive @state field (declared `name: { ... state: true }`) whose name matches `transientStatePatterns` and is NOT assigned in the surface's `settleTransients()` body — unless `<Surface>.<field>` is in `settleAllowlist` with a reason. This makes 'forgot to settle a transient' a build failure, closing the asymmetry where only the destroy direction was gated.

settleAllowlist.why: Reset by LogSurface.unbind() (called from disconnectedCallback) which sets error=null + connectionState='idle' — the channel re-binds and re-derives state on reconnect, so a separate settleTransients reset is redundant.
